topic: Argument hierarchy — are the top kill-shots actually our strongest arguments?
overall_assessment: The current kill-shot hierarchy (Southern Hemisphere distances, GPS accuracy/relativity, Gaia astrometry) is sound but not optimal. These three are strong, but they are ALL external-data arguments vulnerable to the 'you're assuming the standard model is correct' objection. Phase 1 review has surfaced significantly stronger candidates: arguments that use the dome's OWN geometry against it and cannot be escaped through parameter adjustment or exotic refraction. Specifically: Schumann self-contradiction (WIN-002), tidal pattern geometry (WIN-045/046/049/050/051), gravity at rim (WIN-067), and sun/firmament collision are self-contained, mathematically rigorous, and rely on dome-stated parameters only. The current kill-shot order buries these until Section 4.5 — the 8th of 10 tabs. A dome defender who stops after the WIN table (tab 4) has encountered only external falsifications without ever seeing the internal contradiction arguments that are harder to escape. Recommendation: restructure the document so that self-contradiction arguments appear in Part 4 alongside (or before) external falsifications, making the kill-shot section itself more forceful.

current_kill_shots.structure: All three are Part 4 (Falsification Tests) — external data arguments showing dome predictions fail against published observations
current_kill_shots.vulnerability: Dome defender can argue: 'Your measurements are from globe-model satellites, collected into globe-model datasets. We dispute the assumptions behind the data.' This is not a good objection, but it's rhetorically available.

stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.why_stronger: Uses dome's own H(r) function (8537×exp(−r/8619)) against it. Computing the Schumann quarter-wave formula f=c/(4H_avg) on the dome's own geometry gives 22-35 Hz. Observed 7.83 Hz. The dome borrows the 10.59 Hz from Schumann's 1952 GLOBE formula, then blames the gap on 'aetheric damping.' This is internal contradiction — the dome's physics predicts the wrong frequency using its own geometry. Cannot be escaped: if you use your own H(r), you get the wrong answer. If you use the globe's formula, you're admitting the globe is correct. Phase 1 review rates confidence 0.95 on this.
stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.current_placement: Section 4.5.1, Internal Contradictions tab (8th of 10 tabs)
stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.audience_reach: Low — requires reading to tab 8, after 67 WIN summaries
stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.escape_paths_for_dome: Dome could claim the full Maxwell cavity solution is more complex than quarter-wave formula, requiring numerical methods. But it provides no numerical solution, and any reasonable averaging gives 22+ Hz, far from 7.83 Hz. Hard to escape cleanly.

stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.why_stronger: The dome's local moon at 2,534 km produces an inverse-square force field. The near-side force is 99.4% stronger than far-side (dome's own radius and moon distance). This produces ONE sharp spike under the moon, not TWO equal bulges. The dome's geometry is geometrically incompatible with the observed semidiurnal tide pattern — this is a mathematical, not observational fact. Dome cannot claim 'we never made a tidal force prediction' because the model includes the moon and claims it explains tides. The tidal constituent periods (M2, S2, etc.) are trivial arithmetic — any model with the known lunar/solar periods gets them. But the PATTERN is physics, and dome's physics fails. Phase 1 review rates confidence 0.95.
stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.current_placement: Section 4.5.2, Internal Contradictions tab (8th of 10 tabs)
stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.audience_reach: Low — same structural problem as Schumann
stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.escape_paths_for_dome: Dome could invoke an undefined 'aetheric tide-generating mechanism' that doesn't follow inverse-square law. But it states the moon's gravity influences tides (using gravitational language throughout), so this escape contradicts its own stated model. Moreover, any 'aetheric' mechanism must produce the observed two-bulge pattern — the dome never derives such a mechanism.

stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.why_stronger: The dome's H(r) function directly predicts gravity variation. If gravity is proportional to H(r) (the dome's own stated physics), then g_rim/g_pole = H(rim)/H(pole) = 837/8537 = 0.098 (90% variation). GRACE and Swarm satellites WITHIN the dome's alleged firmament (at 400 km altitude) measure 0.53% variation. The dome is wrong by 170×. This is not curve-fitting or interpretation — it is a hard numerical prediction failure using the dome's own H(r) function. Satellites are INSIDE the dome cavity (below 9,086 km firmament), not external observers. The self-contradiction is airtight. Phase 1 review found one critical hole (satellite altitude phrasing) but the core logic survives. Confidence 0.94.
stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.escape_paths_for_dome: Dome could claim H(r) is not the right variable for gravity, or invoke a 'toroidal sump node' that cancels the field. But no such mechanism is defined in dome literature (search of CLAUDE.md and code finds no equations for 'sump node'). Post-hoc invocation of an undefined mechanism is not science.

stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.why_stronger: The dome's inject_ai_layer.py states: sun_altitude=5733 km, firmament_height=9086 km. But the sun's apparent size requires it to have radius ~695,700 km (matching the real sun). At 5,733 km altitude, the dome sun would be at distance d=5733 km from northern hemisphere observers. The apparent angular diameter would be θ=d_sun/d = 695700/5733 ≈ 121 radians ≈ 7000 degrees — the sun would appear nearly 7,000 times larger than it actually does. This is absurd. More technically: the dome sun cannot fit inside the cavity while maintaining realistic angular size. It would collide with the firmament, or the geometry is physically impossible. This is not an observational argument — it's a consistency argument using dome-stated parameters only. Phase 1 review partially addresses this via solar angular diameter (Section 4.8) but doesn't explicitly compute the sun's radius constraint. Confidence 0.90 if formalized.
stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.current_placement: Mentioned in Part 4.8 (solar angular diameter) but not as a self-contained geometric argument
stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.audience_reach: Moderate — Part 4.8 is in the falsification section, more visible than 4.5
stronger_arguments_not_in_kill_shots.escape_paths_for_dome: Dome could define the 'sun' as a small light source with angular size maintained by refraction (n(r) makes it appear larger). But then the sun is not a physical object — it's a mirage. This undermines the model's physical realism. If the sun is a refraction illusion, what about the moon? The stars? The entire firmament becomes metaphysical.

holes_found.description: Current kill-shot order privileges external falsification over self-contradiction. Parts 4.1-4.3 (Southern Hemisphere, GPS, Gaia) are external-data arguments: 'here is what satellites measure, and it contradicts the dome.' These are powerful, but they invite the objection: 'You're using globe-model measurements and globe-model data processing — how do we know your instruments are correct?' A dome defender's most robust counter-narrative is: 'They're measuring through aetheric refraction and misinterpreting it as globe evidence.' The external arguments cannot fully escape this because they depend on trusting satellite data and data-processing pipelines. By contrast, self-contradiction arguments (Schumann, tides, gravity) use DOME-STATED GEOMETRY and dome-stated parameters to show the dome refutes itself. These are much harder to escape: the dome defender would have to claim 'our own H(r) function is wrong' or 'our own moon distance is wrong,' which is incoherent. The document's architecture (external falsifications in Parts 4.1-4.3, internal contradictions in Part 4.5) buries the stronger arguments.
holes_found.recommendation: Restructure Part 4 to front-load self-contradiction arguments. One option: (4.1) Self-Contradictions (Schumann, tides, gravity, sun/firmament), (4.2) External Falsifications (Gaia, GPS, seismic), (4.3) Aetheric Refraction as universal escape hatch. This puts the dome-refutes-itself arguments where readers encounter them first, before the external data arguments. The reading order would be: 'Even if we trust only dome-stated parameters, the dome fails' → 'External data confirms the failure' → 'When cornered, dome invokes undefined aetheric refraction.' Alternatively, keep the current order but add a 'Why self-contradiction arguments are stronger' explanatory paragraph at the start of Section 4.5, explicitly noting that these arguments cannot be escaped by claiming data misinterpretation.

holes_found.description: Three of the four external kill-shots (Southern Hemisphere, Gaia, GPS) are vulnerable to the 'you're measuring from a biased frame of reference' objection. A dome defender can argue: 'GPS satellites use WGS84 globe coordinates; Gaia uses heliocentric frame with globe distance assumptions; Southern hemisphere routes are measured in globe km.' The review anticipates the GPS objection (Section 4.2, 'Anticipated objection') and correctly refutes it — but the refutation (relativity is a physical effect, not a software convention) is subtle and requires some physics background. A lay reader may not grasp why the objection is wrong. Meanwhile, the self-contradiction arguments (Schumann, tides, gravity) do NOT depend on any external frame of reference — they use dome-stated parameters only. A dome defender cannot claim 'you're measuring from the wrong frame' when the arguments derive purely from the dome's own H(r) function and orbital parameters. The current kill-shot order prioritizes the more-vulnerable arguments.
holes_found.recommendation: Make explicit in the Overview or Evaluation Guide: 'The strongest arguments are those that use only the dome's own stated parameters and geometry — not external observations that could be disputed as measured from a biased frame. Arguments that derive contradictions from the dome's own equations are harder to escape than arguments from external data.' Then highlight which kill-shots are dome-self-contained and which depend on external measurements. This sets reader expectations and prepares them to weight self-contradiction arguments more heavily.

holes_found.description: The sun/firmament collision argument is underdeveloped. The dome states: sun_altitude = 5,733 km, firmament_height = 9,086 km (from inject_ai_layer.py, documented in CLAUDE.md). The sun must have radius ~696,000 km to produce its observed angular size (0.5 degrees). A sphere of radius 696,000 km at altitude 5,733 km would extend from 5,733 km altitude to −690,267 km below ground — it would crush through the disc. This is a geometry impossibility using dome-stated parameters. Part 4.8 addresses solar angular diameter as a distance argument ('sun at 5,733 km appears 50% larger than observed'), but it doesn't explicitly formalize the sun-size problem: the dome cannot simultaneously maintain a distant-sun angular size AND fit the sun inside its cavity. This is a stronger argument than it currently appears.
holes_found.recommendation: Expand Part 4.8 (Solar Angular Diameter) or create a new subsection (4.8b) explicitly addressing the sun's physical size constraint. Derive: 'If the sun appears 0.5 degrees in angular size at 5,733 km distance, its actual radius must be R_sun = d × tan(θ/2) ≈ 5,733 × tan(0.25°) ≈ 25 km. But solar radiation spectrum (measured by SOHO and STEREO) implies a blackbody of ~5,778 K emitting like a sphere of radius 696,000 km. A 25 km object at 5,733 km altitude emits orders of magnitude less radiation than observed. The dome model cannot satisfy both constraints: visible angular size AND measured radiation. Resolving this requires invoking undefined aetheric refraction or accepting the sun is not a physical object — either way, the dome's realism fails.' This argument is self-contained and devastating.

holes_found.description: Schumann resonance self-contradiction is presented in detail (Part 4.5.1) but is NOT featured in the headline kill-shots (Parts 4.1-4.3). This is a strategic error. Schumann is arguably the single most devastating self-contradiction: (1) dome's own H(r) predicts 22-35 Hz, (2) observed 7.83 Hz, (3) dome borrows 10.59 Hz from globe formula, (4) gap blamed on aetheric damping, (5) 'damping' is undefined post-hoc invention. The only way to escape is to claim: 'H(r) isn't right for Schumann, we need full Maxwell cavity solution.' But then why claim any prediction at all? If the simple formula fails, the dome should show the full solution — which it hasn't. The confidence (0.95) is highest of any self-contradiction. Why is this not in the headline kill-shots?
holes_found.recommendation: Consider making Schumann the #1 kill-shot (ahead of Southern Hemisphere). The argument is: (a) dome's own H(r) predicts wrong frequency, (b) using dome-only parameters, (c) no external data needed, (d) no refraction escape available that preserves the model's physical coherence. This is the killshot-est kill-shot. The current ordering (SH, GPS, Gaia) is safer for caution but weaker for force.

holes_found.description: The three tidal WINs (045, 046, 049, 050, 051) represent ONE geometric argument stated five times. A dome defender will note: 'You criticize us for counting M2/S2/K1/O1/N2 as five predictions, but you treat them as five self-contradictions.' The HOL-TAXONOMY review already flags this (confidence 0.95 that the group is one argument). The current kill-shots don't mention tides, so this isn't an immediate problem. But if we were to promote tidal geometry to a headline kill-shot, we'd need to acknowledge that it's one argument with five instantiations.
holes_found.recommendation: If tidal geometry enters the headline kill-shots, prefix it with: 'Five tidal constituent WINs (045-051) share one underlying geometric contradiction: the dome's local moon at 2,534 km distance produces inverse-square force with 99.4% variation in magnitude, incompatible with observed two-bulge semidiurnal pattern. These five WINs are distinct only in the specific tidal constituent (M2, S2, etc.); the underlying argument is one.' This preempts the double-counting objection.

holes_found.description: Gravity at rim (WIN-067) has a critical hole flagged in Phase 1 review: the phrasing 'satellites orbit above the dome' is inaccurate. Swarm and GRACE orbit at 250-500 km — BELOW the dome's alleged 9,086 km firmament. The satellites are inside the dome cavity, testing the dome's own gravity field directly. This is actually a STRONGER argument than 'external measurement from above,' but the text currently weakens it. The hole is marked critical in Phase 1, yet it remains unfixed in wins.json. This suggests either the Phase 1 finding wasn't applied, or the impact isn't understood.
holes_found.recommendation: This is actionable: apply the Phase 1 fix. Change the satellite altitude language in WIN-067 detail_evidence to: 'Swarm and GRACE satellites orbit at 250-500 km altitude — well below the dome's alleged firmament of 9,086 km. They measure the dome's own gravity field directly. If gravity ∝ H(r), the dome predicts g_rim/g_pole = 0.098 (90% variation). Observed: 0.0053 (0.53% variation). The dome's prediction fails by 170× within its own cavity.' This makes the self-contradiction even more airtight.

holes_found.description: The current kill-shot structure (SH distances, GPS, Gaia, then sections 4.4-4.9 on satellite imagery, seismic, gravity, stellar motion, lunar ranging, Lagrange points) creates a pacing problem. Sections 4.4-4.9 are longer and equally compelling but appear AFTER Gaia, which is visually the climax. A reader encountering 'Gaia astrometry — 1.8 billion stars, parallax directly implies distance' might think 'that's it, we win, the dome is dead' and stop reading. But the following six sections (satellite imagery, seismic, gravity, proper motion, lunar ranging, Lagrange points) are individually devastating and collectively show the dome is incompatible with EVERY major astronomical test. The structure doesn't reflect the cumulative force of the argument.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider reorganizing Part 4 into two tiers: (Tier 1) Kill-shots — the 2-3 arguments that ALONE are sufficient to falsify the dome (currently SH, GPS, Gaia). (Tier 2) Confirming falsifications — additional independent tests (satellite imagery, seismic, gravity, etc.). Label them clearly so readers understand that Tier 2 is confirmation, not new refutations. Alternatively, reorder Tier 1 by strength: Schumann (dome self-refutes), Tides (dome geometry incompatible), Gravity (dome's own H(r) wrong), then external falsifications. Then Tier 2 becomes 'independent confirmation from different domains.'

holes_found.description: The Aetheric Refraction section (4.9) is in the right place conceptually (explaining why escapes don't work) but is presented as a 'conclusion' about escape hatches rather than as a kill-shot itself. However, the very FACT that the dome requires an undefined refraction function with no independent measurement IS a fatal flaw. A model that invokes a free function to explain any observation is unfalsifiable. This is not a rebuttal to a dome defender objection — it's a primary argument that the dome is not science. The current presentation ('Aetheric refraction: the universal escape hatch') positions it as secondary, when it should be primary: 'The dome's reliance on an unfalsifiable, undefined refraction function for six+ WINs disqualifies the entire model from scientific consideration.'
holes_found.recommendation: Consider moving the Aetheric Refraction argument earlier in Part 4, perhaps as a separate subsection (4.3b or 4.4) before external falsifications. The logic: 'Before we even test external data, the dome's internal framework is compromised: it relies on invoking an undefined correction function (aetheric refraction) to accommodate any observation. This is the definition of an unfalsifiable model. Furthermore, [examples of WINs depending on it]. Given this methodological flaw, the external falsifications below are the confirmation of a verdict already determined by principle.' This inverts the current structure from 'here's external evidence' to 'here's why the model is unfalsifiable, and here's confirmation.'

holes_found.description: The kill-shot structure currently implies the three are equally strong, but they're not. Gaia astrometry (1.8 billion stars, parallax, measured to microarcsecond precision) is hard to dismiss because it rests on elementary trigonometry. GPS (relativity is a physical effect on atomic clocks) requires some physics knowledge to refute. Southern Hemisphere distances (V13 Finsler metric has unpublished scaling functions, hard to audit) is the most technical and hardest for a lay reader to follow. The current presentation treats them as equals, but they have different vulnerabilities to different audiences.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider adding a brief 'Why each kill-shot is hard to escape' summary: 'Gaia astrometry (kill-shot 1): rests on trigonometry and published parallax data, nearly impossible to dismiss without claiming Gaia is lying. GPS (kill-shot 2): requires accepting that relativity is real (atomic clocks don't lie about time), but does require physics background. Southern Hemisphere (kill-shot 3): most technical, relies on auditing unpublished Finsler scaling functions.' This sets reader expectations and helps them understand why Gaia is hardest to escape.

stronger_arguments.description: Prioritize self-contradiction arguments (Schumann, tides, gravity at rim) as the primary kill-shots, with external falsifications (Gaia, GPS, SH distances) as secondary confirmation. Self-contradictions use only dome parameters; external arguments invite frame-of-reference objections.
sources: Phase 1 reviews: WIN-002 (Schumann, 0.95 confidence), WIN-045 (tides, 0.95 confidence), WIN-067 (gravity, 0.94 confidence)
HOL-TAXONOMY review: 'self-contradiction is categorically more damning than external refutation'

stronger_arguments.description: Formalize the sun/firmament collision argument as a standalone kill-shot. The dome cannot simultaneously maintain observed angular solar size AND fit the sun's required physical radius inside a 9,086 km cavity. This is a dome-parameter-only argument, harder to escape than distance-based arguments.
sources: CLAUDE.md: dome core parameters (sun_altitude=5,733 km, firmament_height=9,086 km)
Solar radiation spectrum (SOHO/STEREO data implies blackbody radius ~696,000 km)

stronger_arguments.description: Make Aetheric Refraction not just a 'response to objections' but a primary argumentative wedge. A model that requires an undefined free function to explain any observation is not science by Popperian falsifiability standards. Lead with this principle, then show which WINs depend on unfalsifiable refraction (WIN-016, 017, 026, 033, 056, 065). This argument stands independently of external data.
sources: Philosophy of science: Popper's demarcation criterion

stronger_arguments.description: Add a 'Why you should care about argument hierarchy' section to the Overview or Evaluation Guide. Lay readers don't automatically understand why self-contradiction > external falsification. A brief explanation: 'The dome's objection to external data is always: 'Your data is measured from the wrong frame, through aetheric refraction.' But when the dome's own geometry contradicts itself, there is no frame of reference to hide behind. Both frameworks agree on the math — the dome refutes itself. This is why internal contradictions are the strongest arguments.'
sources: HOL-NARRATIVE review: 'strongest arguments are buried late in the document'

kernel_of_truth.description: The current kill-shots (SH distances, GPS, Gaia) are genuinely strong. Gaia in particular is nearly airtight: 1.8 billion stars, parallax measured to microarcsecond precision, elementary trigonometry. A dome defender cannot credibly dismiss this without claiming massive conspiracy or that all space agencies are wrong. GPS is also strong: relativity is a physical effect on atomic clocks, not a software convention. SH distances are the most technical but still solid.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_needs_restructuring: The kill-shots are strong in isolation, but they are ALL external-data arguments. They are vulnerable (though not fatally) to the objection: 'You're measuring from a biased frame, through aetheric refraction.' The dome has an escape hatch (undefined refraction function) that it uses whenever cornered. By contrast, self-contradiction arguments (Schumann, tides, gravity) do NOT depend on external data — they use only dome-stated parameters. The dome CANNOT use the 'aetheric refraction' escape because both sides of the argument are operating in dome coordinates. A dome defender would have to claim its own H(r) function is wrong — incoherent. The review is strongest when it front-loads self-contradictions, then uses external data as confirmation. Currently it does the reverse.

deeper_analysis:
The Phase 1 curmudgeon reviews have done excellent work identifying and articulating self-contradictions. WIN-002 (Schumann), WIN-045 (tides), WIN-067 (gravity) are all rated with confidence 0.94-0.95 and have tight, rigorous derivations. Yet the document structure buries these in Section 4.5 (Internal Contradictions), after 67 WIN-by-WIN reviews that most readers will skim and after Section 4 (Falsification Tests) which emphasizes external data. A reader who stops at the end of the WIN table (very likely) encounters only the weakest form of the argument: individual rebuttals. A reader who makes it to Section 4 (Falsification Tests) encounters external data. Only a reader who persists to Section 4.5 (Internal Contradictions) encounters the strongest arguments.

This is an inversion of argument strength. The document should be structured like a legal brief: strongest evidence first, supporting evidence later. The current structure is more like a research paper: survey the landscape, examine the details, then draw conclusions. For persuasion, especially against an adversary like dome defenders who are predisposed to dismiss mainstream science, leading with the strongest argument is essential.

The self-contradiction arguments are stronger for several reasons:

1. DOME-PARAMETER-ONLY: They use only the dome's own stated values (H(r), moon distance, sun altitude, disc radius). A dome defender cannot claim 'you're measuring from the wrong frame' — both sides use the same parameters.

2. MATHEMATICAL, NOT OBSERVATIONAL: The tidal argument is pure geometry (inverse-square law + distances). The Schumann argument is pure math (H(r) plugged into quarter-wave formula). The gravity argument is proportionality (g ∝ H(r)). None of these depend on whether GRACE satellites are reliable — the math is what the math is.

3. HARD TO POST-HOC RESCUE: When external data contradicts the dome, the dome says 'aetheric refraction distorts your measurements.' But you can't invoke refraction to rescue a mathematical contradiction. If H(r) predicts 22 Hz and observed is 7.83 Hz, saying 'aetheric refraction explains the gap' doesn't work — refraction affects how WE MEASURE the frequency, but the dome's own math is what it is.

4. SCALABLE TO BROADER AUDIENCE: Gaia astrometry requires understanding parallax, orbital mechanics, and light-years. Schumann resonance requires understanding electromagnetic cavity modes, but the core argument is simple: 'dome's geometry gives wrong frequency.' Tides require understanding inverse-square law, but the core argument is simple: 'local moon makes one spike, not two bulges.' These are more accessible than explaining why GPS relativity falsifies the dome.

The second-order problem is sun/firmament collision. The dome cannot fit a sun of physical radius ~696,000 km (required to match observed angular size and radiation spectrum) inside a cavity of height 9,086 km. This is a pure geometry argument requiring only arithmetic. Yet it's not in the headline kill-shots.

The third-order problem is aetheric refraction unfalsifiability. A model that requires an undefined free function to explain any observation is not falsifiable. This is a philosophical/methodological argument, not a physics argument, but it's devastating: it shows the dome is not science. Currently this appears as Section 4.9 (Aetheric Refraction: The Universal Escape Hatch) at the END of the Falsification section, after readers have already read external data arguments. The structure should put unfalsifiability first, then show it's specifically violated by the dome's reliance on undefined refraction.

What would optimal kill-shot hierarchy look like?

1. DOME SELF-CONTRADICTIONS (dome's own geometry refutes its claims):
   a. Schumann resonance: H(r) predicts 22-35 Hz, not 7.83 Hz
   b. Tidal pattern: local moon produces 1 spike, not 2 bulges
   c. Gravity at rim: H(r) predicts 90% variation, observed 0.53%
   d. Sun/firmament: sun's required radius cannot fit in cavity

2. UNFALSIFIABILITY VIA UNDEFINED REFRACTION (dome's methodology is unscientific):
   a. Aetheric refraction is defined as free function n(r) with no independent measurement
   b. Six+ WINs depend on it to escape falsification
   c. Model that fits any observation is not testable

3. EXTERNAL FALSIFICATIONS (independent data confirms the self-contradictions):
   a. Gaia parallax: 1.8B stars, geometry incompatible with local sun
   b. GPS relativity: atomic clock corrections require spherical Earth geometry
   c. Southern Hemisphere distances: dome's coordinate system has unexplained 460 km discrepancies
   d. Additional confirmation: satellite imagery (continuous sphere), seismic (layered structure), gravity (GRACE mascons), stellar proper motion, lunar ranging, Lagrange points

This order is: self-refutation → scientific flaw → external confirmation. It's also safest strategically: even if dome defenders dispute external data, the self-contradictions and unfalsifiability arguments stand.

The current document (SH, GPS, Gaia in Part 4.1-4.3, then internal contradictions in 4.5) inverts this. It's still defensible, but it's sub-optimal for persuasive force. The recommendation is major_rewrite because the reordering would require moving significant sections and may affect the PDF/DOCX layout. However, the actual content is already correct — it's just in the wrong order.

One mitigation: if full reordering is too disruptive, add an explicit paragraph at the start of Section 4.5 that says: 'The arguments below are more fundamental than external falsifications, because they use only the dome's own stated geometry. When a model contradicts its own parameters, no external data is needed to prove falsification.'

The deeper question: Is the current order a strategic choice (lead with external data for broader credibility) or an oversight (internal contradictions were developed later in the review process)? The file timestamps and CLAUDE.md version history suggest the document was built as a linear report, then adapted to tabs. Internal contradictions (Part 4.5) were likely added later as deeper analysis. If so, the current tab order reflects the development sequence, not optimal persuasive structure.

specific_concerns.concern: Section 4.1 (Southern Hemisphere Distances) is complex and technical. A lay reader may not fully grasp the V12 vs. V13 Finsler metric debate. Leading with this kills momentum — it reads like 'here's a technical failure' rather than 'here's a crushing objection.' By contrast, Schumann resonance in 4.5.1 is more comprehensible to lay readers: 'dome's geometry predicts 22 Hz, observed 7.83 Hz, dome invents undocumented 'damping' to explain gap.' The same information is more persuasive in a different order.
specific_concerns.impact: High — order affects reader comprehension and persuasion
specific_concerns.fix: Move Schumann (4.5.1) to 4.1, and move SH distances to 4.5.1. Adjust section numbering.

specific_concerns.concern: The kill-shot title 'Gaia Astrometry: Parallax and the Distance to Stars' emphasizes the observational/astronomical aspect. But the real kill-shot is not 'Gaia observed X' — it's 'parallax is elementary trigonometry, and the dome's geometry cannot produce observed parallax.' The current framing puts the burden on trusting Gaia's data. A better framing: 'The dome's local sun at 20,000 km cannot produce observed stellar parallax of 10^-4 arcseconds — a pure geometry argument.' This shifts the burden to the dome's geometry, not to Gaia's reliability.
specific_concerns.fix: Rewrite the Gaia introduction to emphasize the geometric incompatibility, not the observational sophistication.

specific_concerns.concern: WIN-067 (Gravity at rim) has a critical flaw flagged in Phase 1 ('satellites above the dome') that hasn't been fixed. This suggests either: (a) the Phase 1 fix wasn't applied, or (b) the impact isn't understood. If (a), it's an oversight. If (b), the fix should be applied anyway because it actually strengthens the argument ('satellites inside cavity, testing dome's own field'). Either way, it should be corrected before promoting this argument to headline status.
specific_concerns.fix: Apply Phase 1 correction to WIN-067 detail_evidence

citations_verified: Phase 1 WIN reviews: WIN-002 (Schumann), WIN-045 (tides), WIN-067 (gravity), confidence levels cited correctly
CLAUDE.md: dome core parameters and V4.9.6 version history
HOL-NARRATIVE and HOL-TAXONOMY reviews: correctly cite observations about argument structure
